radar.title: Turn every signal into a production question
radar.detail: Does it fit a current channel, have rights-safe footage, and give a viewer a specific reason to stay?

radar.title: Travel footage: first-time participation guides
radar.detail: Use specific questions rather than a full-day recap: what to know, how to participate, or what to skip.

radar.title: Investment channel: build repeatable proof formats
radar.detail: Prioritise explainers with a stable host and track retention before expanding channel count.

briefs.title: Japan Field Notes — Daylight
briefs.focus: Create a usable base library for text-led travel films.
briefs.summary: Generate calm, instrumental-only editorial cues. Select the best two for future formal recreation under a production license.
prompts: Instrumental only. 90 BPM. Editorial travel score for a quiet Tokyo food-and-walking film. Warm Rhodes, muted nylon guitar, brushed percussion, soft upright bass, natural room tone. The first 3 seconds are sparse for a map pin, then a gentle forward motion. No vocals or dramatic drop.

Instrumental only. 88 BPM. A clean urban morning cue for a travel guide. Soft marimba, light bass pulse, minimal hand percussion and no lead melody. 30 seconds, loop-friendly, leave 1.5 seconds of quiet at the start for a location card.

Instrumental only. 92 BPM. Dinner-table travel film cue. Subtle electric piano, brushed snare, warm bass and a discreet 8-bar lift after 12 seconds. No vocals, no artist references, clean two-second ending.
